context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{Lemma} \label{lemmalagrangianR3} Let $\tau,\tau'\in[1,\infty)$ and~$\vec x,\vec{x}'\in S^2$. We denote the angle between~$\vec{x}$ and~$\vec{x}'$ by~$\vartheta \in [0, \pi]$ (i.e.\ $\cos \vartheta = \vec{x}\cdot \vec{y}$). Then the operator product $F_\tau(\vec x)F_{\tau'}(\vec x')$ has the eigenvalues \begin{equation}\label{evals_product} \lambda_{1,2}=\frac{1}{4}\: \Big(1+\tau\tau'\cos\vartheta \pm\sqrt{ (1+\tau \tau'\cos \vartheta)^2-(\tau^2-1)(\tau'^2-1) }\Big) \:. \end{equation} Moreover, \begin{equation}\label{lagrangian_R3} {\mathcal{L}} \big((\tau, \vec x), (\tau', \vec x') \big) = \frac1{8} \Big( \big( 1+\tau\tau'\cos\vartheta \big)^2-(\tau^2-1)(\tau'^2-1) \Big) \,\big( 1- \chi_{[\vartheta_-,\vartheta_+]}(\vartheta) \big) \:, \end{equation} where $ \chi_{[\vartheta_-,\vartheta_+]}$ is the characteristic function of the interval defined by \begin{equation}\label{eq:critical_angles} \cos\vartheta_\pm=\frac{-1\mp\sqrt{(\tau^2-1)(\tau'^2-1)}}{\tau\tau'} \:. \end{equation} \end{Lemma}

semantic_equivalence_artifact.original_latex_theorem:
Let~$\rho$ be a causally trivial measure with optimal weights~\eqref{civals}.
Assume that every spacetime point~$(\tau_i,\vec x_i)$ in $supp \rho$ is angular lightlike restricted and 
lies on the boundary of the light cone for at least $k\geq 3$ points. Moreover, assume that the radial coordinates of all points are at least
\[ \tau_i\geq\sqrt{\frac{k}{k-2}} \:. \]
Then~$\rho$ is a local minimizer of the causal action principle.

theorem thetamax4
    {tau : ℝ}
    (F : ℝ → ℝ)
    (x y : ℝ)
    (theta : ℝ)
    (htheta : theta = Real.arccos ((x * y) / (Real.sqrt (x * x) * Real.sqrt (y * y))))
    (htheta_max : theta ≤ Real.arccos (1 - 2 / (tau * tau))) :
    F x * F y = (tau * tau / 64) * (1 + Real.cos theta) * (2 - tau * tau * (1 - Real.cos theta)) * Real.asymptotics_isLittleO (fun _ => theta_max - theta)
